methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $750M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Waabi, a Series C-stage autonomous transportation company founded by Raquel Urtasun, operates within a sector represented by 7 private comparables and no public trading peers. The relevant peer universe includes companies such as Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft, though meaningful valuation triangulation with same-stage or adjacent-stage peers proved constrained by dataset depth.

The Stage-Progression method, the sole applicable triangulation approach, anchors analysis to Waabi's $750M+ total capital raised. Applying a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier characteristic of Series C to subsequent-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $1.3B. A peer-median cross-check was not feasible; while 5 same-sector comparables exist, insufficient clustering around narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or comparable valuation tier) prevented reliable median extraction without introducing sector-wide noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.1B to $1.5B, centered at $1.3B. This range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression point estimate. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiple for Waabi's specific market position and the scarcity of transaction-priced transportation-sector peers at comparable maturity. The single-method foundation means revisions would follow empirical evidence of capital deployment efficiency or material shifts in comparable transaction multiples.
